⏺ The probe is armed and sampling every 12s for the next minute. Now I'll raise a real gate on
  myself — and I'll spend it on a decision that genuinely changes the implementation.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 ☐ Pane probe 

For the `blocked` column: when the cheap signals can't prove it either way (agent is mid-turn — 
could be a long tool call, could be a dialog nobody can see), `fleet vitals` can spend one `cmux 
capture-pane` per *ambiguous* agent to settle it. Which default?
 
❯ 1. Probe by default             ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    (Recommended)                 │ label          state     blocked                              │
 2. Cheap by default, --probe     │   codex-citizen  working   no      ← probed: spinner, no      │
   opts in                        │ dialog                                                        │
  3. Probe one-shot, skip in      │   graph-view     ready     yes     ← probed: dialog on pane   │
    --watch                       │   loom-dev       ready     no      ← cheap: turn closed       │
                                  │                                                               │
                                  │ (a healthy fleet probes 1-3 rows, not 16)                     │
                                  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

                                  Notes: press n to add notes
  
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
